raw_evidence: After captivating the nation’s heart as the winner of Britain’s Got Talent 2017, and announcing the imminent release of his debut album ‘Our Generation’ on Syco Music November 17th, multi-instrumentalist, composer and producer, Tokio Myers recently announced a show at The Albert Tokio Myers is totally unique. He’s created a sound all his own, fusing an array of musical influences that have shaped him as an artist, ranging from classical and cinematic to hip-hop, dance and 90s’ soul Tokio grew up in North London before being awarded a coveted piano scholarship at the Royal College of Music. A stint as a studio musician led to recording and touring with some of the biggest names in music including Amy Winehouse, The Police, Kanye West and Mr Hudson. Now those Tokio has recently released the beautiful single ‘Angel’ from his hotly anticipated album ‘Our Generation’ and it is absolutely gorgeous!

Nathaniel Rateliff is a striking, emotive tenor vocalist and songwriter whose work runs the gamut from simple folk and Americana to roots rock & roll to vintage rhythm & blues. He and his band the Night Sweats are based in Denver, Colorado. After fronting two earlier bands, Ratel Rateliff had begun writing songs that required a band, so he formed the Night Sweats. Their rowdy retro sound was deeply influenced by vintage soul, R&B and gospel traditions, Van Morrison and the Band. A formidable live unit with a reputation for stage-quaking shows, they signed Nathaniel Rateliff & the Night Sweats’ self-titled debut album was issued in August 2015, preceded by appearances at the Newport Folk Festival and The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon. They toured globally in support of the album. While still on the road, they issued A Little S Be sure to catch Nathaniel Ratecliff and The Night Sweats live at Albert Hall on the 14th April 2018!

He’s created a sound all his own, fusing an array of musical influences that have shaped him as an artist, ranging from classical and cinematic to hip-hop, dance and 90s’ soul Tokio grew up in North London before being awarded a coveted piano scholarship at the Royal College of Music. A stint as a studio musician led to recording and touring with some of the biggest names in music including Amy Winehouse, The Police, Kanye West and Mr Hudson. Over the years Danilo Plessow aka. Motor City Drum Ensemble has carved himself a reputation as one of the finest selectors in the game. With headline sets at esteemed festivals such as Dekmantel and Dimensions, and a string of acclaimed releases on his own MCDE imprint, Danilo is Kamaal Williams is a musician of many talents. Co-founder of Yussef Kamaal – Jazz FM’s Best Breakthrough Act of 2017 and championed by the likes of Gilles Peterson, Benji B and Alexander Nut – Their debut album Black Focus remains a cornerstone of scores of DJs’ record bags acros Dele Sosimi is a Nigerian-British musician who has in the past worked with with Afrobeat pioneers Fela Kuti and Tony Allen. Performing as part of a 15-piece orchestra, Dele takes on an extensive touring schedule and has breathed new life into modern day Afrobeat. Fun fact: The pi Chicago selector Rahaan is a real DJs’ DJ, well-versed in the art of dropping disco, boogie and soul, and a consistent presence behind the decks since the 90s. A key contributor to Marcel Vogel’s edit-focussed imprint Lumberjacks in Hell, many consider Rahaan to be the connecting The multi-talented Kamaal Williams returns to the stage as alter-ago Henry Wu, his solo moniker. As a producer, his output has reached familiar labels such as 22a, Eglo Records, and Bradley Zero’s Rhythm Section. The Olllam is a band that fuses Irish traditional music with the modern jazz/post-rock energy of Detroit. Led by uilleann piper John McSherry, the band features Joe Dart (Vulfpeck), Michael Shimmin, Ross Ainslie, Seán O’Meara, and Joe Hettinga Following their acclaimed album elllegy (RTÉ Radio 1 Best Group), The Olllam has collaborated with GRAMMY-nominated producer Zedd, shared the stage in Belfast with actor Anthony Boyle (Netflix’s House of Guinness), and recently completed a landmark U.S. tour in November 2025 From ancient airs to future soundscapes, The Olllam is not just carrying the tradition forward—they’re reinventing it

“Super funky… full of huge grooves, sax solos and lyrics that will make you smile.” — MusicRadar Dynamic jazz-funk saxophonist and bandleader Sam Greenfield announces UK dates for June 2026 – Tickets on sale now! With roots playing alongside elite artists like Cory Wong, Ed Sheeran, John Legend, Mariah Carey, and Joss Stone, Sam and his band deliver a live show that’s equal parts virtuosic musicianship and infectious joy On stage, Sam and his band fuse jazz, funk, and fusion into an electrifying experience — from blazing horn lines to groove-laden, high-energy arrangements that have audiences dancing, laughing, and fully immersed Sam’s shows are anything but ordinary, leaving audiences buzzing long after the final note. With his fifth album in the works, he’s sure to cement his place as one of the most exciting musicians on the scene today

After a quarter of a century, Reid Anderson and Dave King feel like their statement as The Bad Plus has been made. The 2026 Farewell Tour marks the final chance for fans to experience this iconic group live and celebrate one of the most influential and adventurous ensembles in mo The Bad Plus will perform 2 shows at Band on the Wall on Thursday 2 July Since their debut in 2000, The Bad Plus have pushed the boundaries of what jazz music could and should be. From their earliest days, their indelible compositions coexisted with an unconventional array of covers from outside of the strictly-policed jazz bubble: everything from Orn Line-up: Reid Anderson, Dave King, Ben Monder, Chris Speed

MANCHESTER JAZZ FESTIVAL AT BAND ON THE… From fusion pioneers and soul-jazz stars, to high energy afro-jazz and even a club night of global grooves – Explore our full programme for Manchester Jazz Festival below Individual gig tickets are on sale now, plus free shows in The Copper Bar Releasing their eponymous debut album in 1981 at the height of the fusion era, Yellowjackets draw upon a history-spanning mélange of jazz movements, steeped in funk grooves and chamber music sophistication, Yellowjackets have evolved to become a genre unto themselves Signed to Brownswood recordings, enigmatic East London songstress Bel Cobain merges jazz, r&b and rap with a peaceful and self-reflective slant, to create boundary-transcending music powered by radical ethos, poetic lyrics, and ethereal melodies. For fans of: Greentea Peng, ELIZA Somewhere Soul presents Cotonete – Sat 23 May | TICKETS

Soul queens, jazz innovators and global icons – This International Women’s Day, we’re shining a spotlight on some of the artists who’ve played a defining role in the genres that sit at the heart of our programming Throughout the rest of Women’s History Month, we’ll be celebrating the women who shape Band on the Wall, from those working behind the scenes to trailblazing artists heading to our stage later this year Follow along as we celebrate the past, present and future of women in music Nina Simone expanded the possibilities of jazz, musically, emotionally and politically. Drawing on her classical training, she merged jazz with blues, folk and soul, reshaping vocal expression in the process. Her songs confronted civil rights and injustice with clarity and contro The Queen of Salsa, Celia brought explosive energy and unmistakable power to Afro-Cuban music. Her bold performances and vibrant voice helped globalise salsa, celebrating Caribbean identity while shaping dance music culture for generations Blending psychedelic soul with jazz, hip-hop and experimental production, Erykah Badu helped define the neo-soul movement through her distinctive voice and artistic independence. As a key figure within the Soulquarians collective, she influenced a wave of artists through music gr

Working with the eminent composer and conductor Kate Pearson and celebrated director Jonathan Ainscough, our 50-strong Community Chorus has written a brand-new opera to perform alongside our students and local comm This year’s production is a tale of loss, magic, and the destructive power of nature and human relationships inspired by the history of Manchester’s lost waterways . Step into the national museum of democracy after hours for a bold live music experience Wander around the galleries as RNCM musicians perform powerful new works inspired by the museum’s collection, created in collaboration with Mancunian poet and playwright Louise Wallwein. These next-gen artists, studying in the city of Peterloo and the Pankhursts, continue the spi United through camaraderie and creativity, they add the living soundtrack to the stories of resistance, reform, and rebellion that are shared by People’s History Museum Content warning: references to discrimination, grief, violence, suicide and mild offensive language in the context of social justice and protest. Under 14s must be accompanied by an adult Gallery One Duets written by RNCM Composers, inspired by exhibits in the gallery: Annah George With You I Live Ella Laughton Newport Rising Kexin Chen The Shape of a Public Voice Rafik Harrington black eggs Joaquin Garces Caamano Nanas de la Cebolla (‘Lullaby of the Onion’) Gallery Two Two repeating 15-minute sound installations by RNCM Composers, inspired by Re/Assemble, a temporary exhibition on the 1988 Section 28 protest in Manchester: Leonard Nucinkis And we did not speak out enough Thomasine O’Sullivan and Luca Mellor Girl

The Only King is acclaimed as the first original Cantonese biblical musical to present the story of the Book of 1 Samuel in its entirety. The production follows the life of King Saul, from his anointing and rise to the throne to his gradual downfall through pride and disobedience First staged in 2005, The Only King enjoyed multiple sold-out performances in Hong Kong and is widely regarded as a landmark work in Chinese Christian musical theatre To mark the 20th anniversary of its premiere, The Only King was presented in London and Cambridge in January 2026, where it received enthusiastic responses and strong audience acclaim. Building on this success, the production will embark on a UK tour in September 2026, with perfo Performed in Cantonese with English subtitles Suitable for audiences aged 3 and above. Children under the age of 12 must be accompanied by an adult * These tickets include an exclusive souvenir

Young Musician’s Summer School . Young Musician’s Summer School. . Book here . . . . . . . Friday | 03.07.2026 . . . World of Music . Share . . . . --> . . We’re inviting young people to explore music for film and compose their own soundtrack this summer! Whether your child already attends our Saturday Music Club , or just wants to try something new, our Young Musician’s Summer School is suitable for all abilities! . . . . . Across this 5 day course, we’ll be exploring music for film, and working on composing music for the silent film ‘A Trip to the Moon’. The course will conclude with a live performance soundtracking the film for family and friends. . . . . We welcome all abilities and instruments to take part in this course. You can bring your own instruments, but we have keyboards, guitars, bass, violins, drums and percussion on site which can be used if you don’t have an instrument.. . . Snacks and drinks provided, and pizzas on the final day. Please bring your own packed lunch to sessions Monday-Thursday.. . Dates: Monday 17th – Friday 21st August. Times: 10:00-16:30 Monday-Thursday, then a performance for friends and family on Friday at 13:00 (students will be expected to come in at 10:00 sharp on the Friday).. Ages: 12-18 years old. Cost: £150 for the full course. Bursaries are available for those on lower income – please email learning@bandonthewall.org to arrange this.. . Spaces are limited – Book here . . . Please email learning@bandonthewall.org if you have any questions about this course
